evidence_basis_summary: Both sources focus on delegation within a pre-existing interview corpus; no discussion of scope/inclusion adjudication for the analysis universe.
notes: Upgraded low to high: sources provide enough detail to confidently rule this role out.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither paper pressures causal inference or mechanism claims; focus is interpretive QDA coding, not causal model discipline.
notes: Upgraded low to high: scope of sources clearly excludes causal/mechanism work.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_P1 shows participants guard interpretive authority over codes/themes and C018_S1 documents epistemic negotiation and IRR concerns, directly pressuring construct and perspective boundaries.
notes: Upgraded low to high: multiple direct passages on codebook ownership, subjective interpretation, and inter-rater reliability.

evidence_basis_summary: Sources mention multimodal data (audio, video, non-verbal cues) as underused but do not frame corpus diversity as a human-guarded role; indirect relevance only.
notes: Upgraded low to medium: multimodal discussion in C018_P1 touches corpus breadth in passing but does not adjudicate the role.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_S1 explicitly calls for frameworks, responsible integration, version/tool standards and IRR reporting; C018_P1 foregrounds workflow design, authorship distinction, and data confidentiality protocols.
notes: Upgraded low to high: both papers design/advocate explicit QDA workflow and disclosure architectures.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source concerns transporting simulated/forecast results across settings; scope is in-project coding delegation.
notes: Upgraded low to high: out of scope confirmed by full-text review.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_P1 repeatedly stresses AI's lack of contextual understanding and the interpretive value of tone/non-verbal cues; C018_S1 highlights context-sensitive work and missed subtle judgments by LLMs.
notes: Upgraded low to high: context/interpretation is a central theme across both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_P1 centers ownership, authorship distinction, and professional identity; C018_S1 discusses researcher autonomy, approval gates, and responsible deployment.
notes: Upgraded low to high: ownership/authorship/autonomy explicit contribution of both papers.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_P1 shows participants insist on human-led code/theme definition; C018_S1 describes codebook development and deductive/inductive boundary setting as a retained human task.
notes: Upgraded low to high: codebook/category-boundary work explicitly discussed.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_S1 flags AI risks of privileging dominant perspectives and missing Global South/minority framings; C018_P1 surfaces shallow agreement risk where researchers converge on AI suggestions. Indirect support for preserving disagreement.
notes: Upgraded low to medium and observable no to partial: both papers raise subgroup/perspective-preservation concerns without framing it as a formal role. See notes for status change rationale.

evidence_basis_summary: Sources do not address transporting results across populations or design counterfactuals; scope is workflow delegation.
notes: Upgraded low to high: out of scope confirmed.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_P1 references trust-calibration literature (Ma et al.) and uses non-LLM models (BERTopic/Word2Vec) for transparency, but this is benchmarking of trust not simulator calibration.
notes: Upgraded low to medium: partial/tangential relevance noted but not a core role in either paper.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_P1 notes AI's limits in interpretive interview settings and the value of researcher presence; data collection is adjacent but not the primary focus of either paper.
notes: Upgraded low to medium: interviewer-in-field role implied but not directly studied.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_P1 repeatedly reports researchers defending contextual/tacit interpretation of transcripts; C018_S1 documents that LLMs miss subtle contextual judgments and that context-sensitivity is central.
notes: Upgraded low to high: direct, repeated textual evidence for context-witness function.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_S1 identifies AI potential in data pre-processing and onboarding and C018_P1 notes AI automating repetitive tasks, but neither studies first-pass abstract/title screening per se.
notes: Upgraded low to medium: adjacent shrinkable tasks discussed but not the screener role exactly.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: Both papers emphasize QDA lacks external ground truth and participants reject AI as primary coder; routine high-consensus coding is explicitly not the QDA setting.
notes: Upgraded low to high: sources directly argue this role is not applicable to interpretive QDA.

evidence_basis_summary: C018_S1 mentions AI use for writing and summarization in adjacent research tasks; C018_P1 restricts AI to coding assistance and does not examine drafting.
notes: Upgraded low to medium: peripheral mention only, not the study focus.
